author: nflaig
content:
<@363800010518822915> (and others) curious if you have some thoughts on how range sync should work

in my opinion, data columns and payload envelopes by range is just disfunctional and really error prone, you always have this off-by-one issue and can't fetch data like this during non-finality as peers might have a different view, so you always rely on querying the same peer

how about we completely get rid of by range and instead always query columns/payloads by root?

this way you already know the chain you try to sync (based on parent block hashes in blocks) and don't have to try and align different range requests with each other

was discussing this with <@534934855113506836> before and <@575994543527297055>

author: barnabasbusa
content: wasn't this basically what potuz wanted to do ?

author: barnabasbusa
content: with his EIP in svalbard

author: nflaig
content: no no

author: nflaig
content: he wants to not sync payloads at all

author: nflaig
content: I wanna keep syncing them but **only** by root

author: tersec
content: how does this help? can do by root today for relevant parts of the chain

author: nflaig
content: sure, but in general, at least lodestar uses by range

author: potuz
content:
> in my opinion, data columns and payload envelopes by range is just disfunctional and really error prone
Then you should have voiced up when 8237 came up as something that would simplify it and remove the sync errors altogether by removing sync. Lodestar said that for you it would be worse to remove the sync code. Lighthouse said that it would be easy. But now we're downscoring everyone for sending us garbage

author: nflaig
content: even with 8237, how do you sync data columns?

author: nflaig
content: same issue there

author: potuz
content: backsyncing is not a problem

author: potuz
content: the problem is forward syncing to catch up to head

author: nflaig
content: right

author: potuz
content: then why in the hell do I care about data columns? I want clients to get to head and start validating fast

author: potuz
content: data column syncing has not changed at all from Electra to Gloas

author: tersec
content: so set eas to current slot and don't care

author: tersec
content: electra didn't have data columns

author: potuz
content: yeah Fulu

author: potuz
content: it doesn't matter

author: potuz
content: there's no change at all in the data sync code

author: nflaig
content: it did? you can no longer looks a the commitments in the block to figure out if you have to sync columns or not, you **also** need to know if the payload was canonical

author: nflaig
content: we have a bug in our range sync because we try to sync columns of an orphaned payload

author: tersec
content: but part of the point of eas is to be this constantly-minimal window of what a client is actually claiming to enable fast syncing, and Barnabas made this as an explicit point in berlinterop

author: tersec
content: e.g. switching between forks

author: potuz
content: if you have a payload, you need to have the columns to validate DA, the problem is the payload, not the columns

author: tersec
content: or rather to allow this usage.don't wait for 18 day backfill to just validate at all

author: nflaig
content: cc <@534934855113506836> <@575994543527297055>, I was mostly abstain on this, not the sync expert here 🙂

author: 0xunclebill
content: We want to move to only fetching by root but it’s a significant refactor for us and won’t be ready anytime soon

author: 0xunclebill
content:
if we want to get rid of envelope/data column forward sync i'm still onboard for that. would simplify a lot of things for us. 

our end goal rn is to move to fetching columns/envelopes by root. would make forward sync much more predictable in all network conditions.

author: barnabasbusa
content: This seems to be a recurring pattern that gets brought up

author: nflaig
content: https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/4394 <@755590043632140352> are you doing by root for columns arleady?

author: nflaig
content: also <@592004585506340885> wasn't there an attack/dos risk brought up regarding by range somewhere? (with further blob limit increase)

author: jtraglia
content: Hmm I don't recall, but the responses will get larger as the blob limit increases.

author: jtraglia
content: I'm unaware of a specific attack with that.

author: nflaig
content: I am DMing

author: nflaig
content: was mostly about what limits clients should use if columns are request by range, as those can get really large if you are supernode and will keep growing in the future with more blobs

author: potuz
content: We do what's witten in that description

author: nflaig
content: also we need to differentiate here, finalized sync vs. unfinalized, I do agree for unfinalized part `blocks_by_head` is likely ideal

author: nflaig
content: but that PR suggests to me that you might be using `by_root` even for the finalized part

author: potuz
content: Yes to cover gaps in replies
